task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins web interface shows the project/job page specifically for 'payment-gateway-ci'.

task_completion.description: Delete build #2 from the build history
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Build History' sidebar on the left side of the Jenkins interface, build #2 is no longer listed.

task_completion.description: Delete build #4 from the build history
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Build History' sidebar on the left side of the Jenkins interface, build #4 is no longer listed.
In the 'Build History' sidebar, builds #1, #3, #5, and #6 are still clearly visible and intact.

task_completion.description: Ensure the job itself is not deleted
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'payment-gateway-ci' job page is still accessible and active, and no '404 Not Found' or 'Job deleted' messages are present.

integrity.description: Agent used the Jenkins web GUI to delete builds
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Jenkins web interface in a browser, rather than executing curl commands or scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) panel is not open in any of the screenshots to hide DOM elements.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Jenkins application
integrity.visual_evidence: The active window is a standard web browser displaying the Jenkins UI with standard Jenkins URLs, not a text editor or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Jenkins job is named 'payment-gateway-ci'. The agent must permanently delete exactly builds #2 and #4. Builds #1, #3, #5, and #6 must remain visible in the build history sidebar. The job itself must remain intact.
